title: Question 1: Insertion and deletion operations in an array
problem_statement: Insert: shift elements right from the insert position, then place the value. Delete: shift elements left over the deleted position.

title: Question 2: Search for an element using Linear Search and Binary Search
problem_statement: Linear Search works on unsorted data. Binary Search requires the array to be sorted.

title: Question 3: Sort an array using Bubble Sort, Selection Sort, and Insertion Sort
problem_statement: Implement Bubble Sort, Selection Sort, and Insertion Sort algorithms to sort an array. Each function works on its own copy so the three runs are independent.

title: Question 4: Merge two arrays into one
problem_statement: Merge two sorted arrays into one. If both inputs are already sorted, a two-pointer merge produces a sorted result.

title: Question 5: Add and subtract two matrices
problem_statement: Add and subtract two matrices. Both operations require identical dimensions and run applying the operation element-wise.

title: Question 6: Multiply two matrices
problem_statement: Multiply two matrices. Valid only when A's column count equals B's row count.

title: Question 7: Stack operations using an array
problem_statement: Implement stack operations using an array (LIFO structure). `top == -1` means the stack is empty.

title: Question 8: Evaluate a postfix expression using a stack
problem_statement: Scan left to right: push operands; on an operator, pop two operands, apply it, push the result back. The final stack value is the answer.

title: Question 9: Recursion examples (Factorial, GCD, Tower of Hanoi)
problem_statement: Implement recursion examples: Factorial, GCD using Euclidean algorithm, and Tower of Hanoi.

title: Question 10: Simple queue operations using an array
problem_statement: Implement simple queue operations using an array (FIFO structure). This is a linear queue (not circular).
